rules.title: Risky shell execution
rules.description: Flags direct shell execution or dangerous command patterns.
rules.why_it_matters: Running shell commands from third-party skill instructions can execute unreviewed code on the local system.
rules.recommendation: Do not install or run until the commands are manually reviewed in an isolated environment.

rules.title: Remote download or external fetch
rules.description: Flags remote downloads, ZIP fetches, or external installation assets.
rules.why_it_matters: Remote downloads can introduce unreviewed code or payloads from outside the local environment.
rules.recommendation: Verify the source, inspect downloaded content manually, and prefer transparent local installation steps.

rules.title: Credential-related content
rules.description: Flags content that references credentials, authorization, or secrets in a general documentation context.
rules.why_it_matters: Credential-related content deserves manual attention, but a mention of keys or tokens in documentation is not automatically a dangerous request.
rules.recommendation: Review how credentials are obtained, stored, and scoped. Treat this as a manual-review signal, not an automatic blocker.

rules.title: Explicit secret request or exposure guidance
rules.description: Flags direct instructions to provide, paste, export, or expose sensitive credentials.
rules.why_it_matters: Direct secret collection or exposure guidance materially increases the chance of credential compromise.
rules.recommendation: Do not provide or expose secrets until necessity, storage method, and trust boundary are clearly reviewed.

rules.title: Privilege or sensitive access request
rules.description: Flags admin/root requests or access to sensitive directories and system configuration.
rules.why_it_matters: Excessive privilege increases the blast radius of any malicious or mistaken instruction.
rules.recommendation: Prefer least privilege, isolate execution, and reject installation if sensitive access is not clearly justified.

rules.title: Over-broad or unsafe operational claim
rules.description: Flags vague promises that encourage unsafe trust or direct execution.
rules.why_it_matters: Claims such as auto-fix, bypass restrictions, or fully secure can indicate overreach, poor safety posture, or misleading instructions.
exclude_patterns: ^\s*it does not promise to fully secure
rules.recommendation: Treat over-broad claims as a trust warning and require manual review before use.
